SEARCH
Package org.plugin.refactor.code

Class ForEachToWhileVisitor

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.ASTVisitor
org.plugin.refactor.code.ForEachToWhileVisitor
public class ForEachToWhileVisitor extends org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.ASTVisitor
AST visitor responsible for transforming marked EnhancedForStatement nodes into semantically equivalent WhileStatement structures.

The transformation distinguishes between different iteration kinds:

  • Primitive arrays
  • Reference arrays
  • Iterable implementations

Depending on the detected type, the visitor generates either:

  • An index-based while loop (for primitive arrays)
  • An Iterator<T>-based loop (for reference arrays and Iterables)

The transformation preserves execution semantics by:

  • Maintaining evaluation order
  • Ensuring correct element typing (including boxing when required)
  • Introducing casts where necessary
  • Preventing variable name collisions
  • Adding required imports if missing

Only enhanced for statements annotated with the property "change" are transformed.

  • Constructor Details

    • ForEachToWhileVisitor

      public ForEachToWhileVisitor(Set<org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.EnhancedForStatement> markedFors, org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.rewrite.ASTRewrite rewrite, org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.CompilationUnit cu)
      Creates a new visitor that converts marked enhanced-for statements using the provided rewrite context.
      Parameters:
      markedFors - set of loops previously marked for conversion
      rewrite - AST rewriter used to apply structural changes
      cu - compilation unit used for import management

  • Method Details

    • visit

      public boolean visit(org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.EnhancedForStatement node)
      Visits EnhancedForStatement (for-each) nodes to perform their conversion into WhileStatement structures. The process follows these steps:
      1. Validation: Checks if the node is flagged for transformation.
      2. Classification: Determines if the expression is a primitive array, an object array, or a Iterable collection.
      3. Conversion: Dispatches the transformation to specialized methods based on the detected type.
      4. Replacement: Updates the AST with the generated while-loop and any required setup statements (e.g., iterator initialization).
      Overrides:
      visit in class org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.ASTVisitor
      Parameters:
      node - The EnhancedForStatement node to be transformed.
      Returns:
      false to prevent visiting nested nodes that are already being refactored, true if no transformation is performed.

    • endVisit

      public void endVisit(org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.CompilationUnit node)
      Signal to finalize the transformation process when the entire compilation unit has been visited.
      Overrides:
      endVisit in class org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.ASTVisitor
      Parameters:
      node - The CompilationUnit being finished.
